Title: CREATE VIEW
Description:
Define a new SQL view. A view is a virtual table based on the result set of a stored SELECT query.
A view does not physically store data. It is useful to reduce query complexity as it can include joins,
computations, unions, aggregations. It can increase standardisation and reusability and be used to control
data access, provide an abstraction layer between applications and physical tables, simplify reporting.

Limitation: Views are not updatable. The "WITH CHECK OPTION" is accepted for compliance but has no effect.
Recursive views and reference-able views are not supported. Note : An "ORDER BY" clause in the SELECT-query is supported.
See also: COMMENT ON VIEW , COMMENT ON COLUMN , DROP VIEW ).
Associated system tables: information_schema.views , sys.tables where type = 1
Examples:
CREATE [ OR REPLACE ] VIEW [
schema_name
'.' ]
view_name
[ '('
column_name
[,
column_name
] [, ... ] ')' ]
    AS
SELECT_query
[ WITH CHECK OPTION ]
